Resume / Portfolio Main Page

Jae Woong Hwang

ADAS Controls, Embedded Validation & Vehicle Toolchain Engineer

Production ADAS controls, embedded validation, and vehicle-development toolchain experience across commercial trucks, tractors, buses, and fuel-cell platforms.

I connect production-facing ADAS behavior logic, full V-cycle validation workflows, resource-constrained calibration environments, and practical Python/C++/MATLAB toolchains for commercial-vehicle development.

South Korea
jwhwang91@gmail.com
+82-10-5775-1863
Portfolio Source

Target Roles

Commercial-Vehicle ADAS SCC / L2 ADAS Embedded Validation Vehicle Dynamics Production-Facing Behavior Logic MILS/HILS Dynamometer / Real Vehicle CANape / VN1640 XCP / CAN-FD Legacy Workflow MF4 Log Processing MATLAB / Simulink BEV Replay Debugging ONNX ADAS AI Validation Transition Resource-Constrained Tooling

Professional Summary

  • Production-facing commercial-vehicle ADAS controls experience across SCC/L2 behavior logic and release-readiness support.
  • Embedded validation experience across MILS, HILS, dynamometer, and real-vehicle workflows.
  • Resource-constrained vehicle toolchain development using existing field equipment such as CANape, VN1640, MF4 logs, XCP, CAN-FD, Python, C++, and MATLAB/Simulink.
  • Proactive ADAS AI-validation transition platforms that help module owners learn, train, and test learned-model workflows before full Ethernet/HPC infrastructure adoption.

Core Competencies

Control: Vehicle dynamics / Commercial-vehicle ADAS controls / SCC/L2 ADAS behavior logic / Emergency-stop state behavior / Target-selection robustness / Road-curvature estimation / Brake distribution logic

Validation: MILS / HILS / Dynamometer / Real-vehicle validation / Calibration/debugging / Release-readiness support / Log replay validation

Software: Python / C/C++ / MATLAB/Simulink / CANape/VN1640 / XCP over CAN-FD / MF4 preprocessing / ONNX / ADAS AI-validation transition tooling

Professional Experience

Researcher - KAIST Master's Research Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) | 2015.03 - 2017.08
Technical Case Study

Embedded motion-control foundation: designed and validated turret stabilization control for real hardware under moving-platform disturbance.

  • Designed a motion-stabilization controller for a turret mounted on a moving platform, connecting dynamics modeling, control logic, and sensor-fusion concepts.
  • Validated control behavior on actual turret hardware under artificial base-motion disturbance, sensor noise, and repeatable test conditions.
  • Built a control-engineering foundation later reused in vehicle dynamics, estimation, and production ADAS validation work.

Detail: experience/kaist-masters/index.html

Researcher - TCU Application Software Agency for Defense Development (ADD), Ground Technology Research Institute | 2017.09 - 2021.03
Technical Case Study

Full V-cycle TCU productization: developed sensorless shift-control software for the K2 55-ton tracked-vehicle transmission from MILS/HILS to dynamometer and field validation.

  • Developed TCU application software for K2 tracked-vehicle transmission control, including shift logic, driver-intent decision logic, and calibration-related behavior.
  • Built and used hydraulic/mechanical plant models for MILS/HILS validation before hardware, powerpack, and vehicle testing.
  • Validated software through the full V-cycle: MILS, HILS, powerpack dynamometer, and actual vehicle field testing.
  • Supported software verification, parameter correction, calibration, reliability-test response, and vehicle-level issue resolution.

Detail: experience/add-k2-tcu/index.html

Commercial Vehicle ADAS Controls & Validation Engineer Hyundai Motor Company (HMC) | 2021.03 - Present
Technical Case Study

Production commercial-vehicle ADAS controls, validation, and workflow-toolchain experience across trucks, tractors, buses, and fuel-cell platforms.

  • Developed and validated selected production SCC/L2 ADAS behavior logic across HMC commercial-vehicle programs including truck, tractor, bus, ICE, and FCEV variants.
  • Independently developed SCC emergency-stop behavior and production-applied road-radius fusion logic combining steering-intent radius, EBS yaw-rate radius, and conditional lane/camera validity.
  • Supported SCC tuning, FOC tuning, state-transition regulation updates, calibration, regression testing, and release-readiness workflows across MILS, HILS, dynamometer, and real vehicles.
  • Built and used a BEV target-selection validation studio to replay real logs through production TOS/ODP logic, reducing repeated bus-only-lane road-test loops from about 10 trips to about 5 for similar false-detection issues.
  • Built proactive ADAS AI-transition toolchains that reused available CANape/VN1640/MF4/Python/C++ workflows to help module owners learn, train, and test learned-model concepts despite limited Ethernet and Thor/Orin-class AI-compute infrastructure.

Detail: experience/hmc-adas/index.html

Selected Technical Projects

Public-safe technical case studies for validation tooling, AI-assisted implementation, and commercial-vehicle ADAS debugging workflows.

Turning a Legacy Calibration Setup into an ADAS AI Validation Loop Python / C++ / CANape / VN1640 / XCP over CAN-FD / ONNX
Technical Case Study GitHub

Resource-constrained real-vehicle AI validation prototype built from the team’s existing CANape/VN1640 laptop workflow.

Objective: Help commercial-vehicle ADAS module owners explore learned-model validation on real vehicles before full Ethernet/HPC infrastructure adoption by reusing the available CANape/VN1640/laptop calibration workflow.

Impact: Provided a practical stepping stone from legacy calibration to AI validation under limited equipment, enabling model owners to map real vehicle signals, run ONNX/Python model experiments, and learn future validation concepts without waiting for Ethernet interface hardware or dedicated AI compute platforms.

Python C++ XCP CAN-FD ONNX CANape/VN1640 AI Validation Transition

Detail: toolchains/e2e-xcp-bypass/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/realtime-vehicle-ai-validation-studio

ADAS Log-to-AI Workbench Python / MF4 / PyTorch / ONNX / MATLAB Export
Technical Case Study GitHub

CANape/MF4-to-AI workbench for module-owner learning, dataset preparation, and model-artifact export.

Objective: Help ADAS module owners turn familiar MF4 logs into AI-ready datasets and model artifacts through a repeatable preprocess/train/evaluate/export workflow, rather than forcing each engineer to build separate scripts or a full ML platform from day one.

Impact: Lowered the entry barrier for AI experimentation by connecting the existing log-analysis workflow to Python training and ONNX/MATLAB export, giving engineers a practical transition path from conventional signal review to model-behavior learning.

Python MF4 PyTorch ONNX MATLAB Export AI Training Workflow

Detail: toolchains/timeseries-ai-training/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/adas-log-to-ai-workbench

BEV Target Selection Validation Studio MATLAB Script / Simulink / Base Workspace / ADAS Visualization
Technical Case Study GitHub

Production-development BEV replay tool used for TOS/ODP false-target analysis in commercial-vehicle programs.

Objective: Replay real vehicle logs through production TOS/ODP logic to investigate false target selection and reduce repeated real-road trials, especially bus-only-lane scenarios that were difficult to reproduce on demand.

Impact: Used frequently during HMC commercial-vehicle production development; in bus scenarios, reduced repeated road-validation loops from about 10 trips to about 5 for similar issues, cutting 4–5 hour test trips roughly in half before final real-vehicle confirmation.

MATLAB Simulink ADAS BEV TOS/ODP Log Replay

Detail: toolchains/tos-odp-bev-simulator/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/bev-target-selection-validation-studio
